tmj.title: Arthroscopic TMJ Surgery

ent_page.hero_sub: Bipolar radiofrequency devices — RFITT and Plasma — for nasal surgery, skull base, sleep surgery, and laryngeal procedures.
ent_page.gallery_desc: Click any video to view full surgical demonstrations or product presentations. Exclusive content for qualified healthcare professionals.
ent_page.vc_turbilon_desc: Full demonstration of the radiofrequency turbinate ablation procedure.
ent_page.vc_rfhook_desc: Demonstration of the bipolar plasma RF hook for nasal flaps.
ent_page.vc_bisuctor_desc: Bipolar plasma device with irrigation and suction for tonsils, adenoids, and tongue base.
ent_page.turbilon_tagline: Hypertrophic nasal turbinate ablation — deliver the required energy, not an estimate, without over-dosing
ent_page.turbilon_desc1: Bipolar RFITT radiofrequency (Celon type) measures impedance throughout tissue ablation. As impedance rises, energy delivery decreases until it reaches zero. This mechanism ensures no energy overdose, making the procedure safer. Ablation occurs at low temperature, not exceeding 80 degrees Celsius.
ent_page.turbilon_desc2: The probe has a diameter of 1.1mm compared to the traditional 1.8mm of coblation probes. A finer device means less tissue damage, as the insertion point will have a smaller diameter. The Turbilon model is suitable for use in both paediatric and adult patients.
ent_page.turbilon_b1: Impedance measurement — power automatically reduces to zero at optimal ablation
ent_page.turbilon_b2: 1.1mm tip diameter — less tissue damage and bleeding than standard 1.8mm devices
ent_page.turbilon_b3: Suitable for paediatric and adult patients
ent_page.turbilon_b4: Acoustic auto-stop signal — confirms optimal ablation point
ent_page.turbilon_b5: Single-use disposable · Ethylene oxide sterilised · 11cm working length · 45° angulation
ent_page.pvid_t3_sub: Less damage · Less bleeding at insertion
ent_page.rfhook_tagline: Bipolar plasma radiofrequency for nasal flap procedures
ent_page.rfhook_desc1: When creating a nasoseptal flap, protecting the edges is essential. Monopolar energy causes tissue retraction as it generates high temperatures, carbonising tissue and damaging flap edges.
ent_page.rfhook_desc2: Bipolar plasma radiofrequency enables cutting and coagulation without exceeding 80 degrees Celsius. The tissue does not carbonise, there is no retraction, and flap edges are preserved.
ent_page.rfhook_b1: Hook-shaped device for precise technique execution
ent_page.bisuctor_tagline: Malleable tip for bipolar plasma radiofrequency ablation
ent_page.bisuctor_desc1: Bipolar plasma radiofrequency achieves cuts and coagulation at low temperature, not exceeding 70–80 degrees. One challenge with this technology is reaching different areas of the oropharyngeal and nasal anatomy. CoworkDoc has developed the first fully malleable device, breaking through those limitations.
ent_page.bisuctor_desc2: The Bisuctor is available in two models: 12 and 15 French. Indicated for adenoid ablation, palatine tonsils, lingual tonsils, tongue base, nasal coagulation, and more. The device consists of an irrigation tube and a suction tube. The Bisuctor's unique design notably improves aspiration, preventing blockages.
ent_page.bisuctor_b1: Malleable tube that can reach any area of the anatomy
ent_page.bisuctor_b4: Indicated for lingual tonsils, palatine tonsils, adenoids, tongue base, and more
ent_page.cta_heading: Interested in any of these products for your practice?
ent_page.cta_body: Contact us for product information, pricing, and availability in your region. We respond within 1 business day.
ent_page.more_atm_desc: Instruments designed specifically for temporomandibular joint arthroscopy.
ent_page.toast_msg: Interested in using this product in your practice?

tmj_page.hero_sub: Instruments designed specifically for temporomandibular joint arthroscopy. Developed under the guidance of leading maxillofacial surgeons.
tmj_page.gallery_desc: Click any video to view full surgical demonstrations. Exclusive content for qualified healthcare professionals.
tmj_page.vc_featured_desc: TMJ arthroscopy procedure demonstrating the rf-max probe inside the joint cavity. 1.8mm diameter, blunt tip — navigating the confined temporomandibular space.
tmj_page.vc_coming2_desc: More surgical demonstration content coming soon.
tmj_page.rfmax_tagline: Bipolar plasma radiofrequency probe designed specifically for the temporomandibular joint
tmj_page.rfmax_desc1: Before the rf-max, maxillofacial surgeons had to rely on probes developed for other specialties — such as those used in nasal turbinate ablation by ENT surgeons. Using a device not designed for this purpose carries various risks: joint perforation, accidental tearing of healthy tissue, and unintended burns from accidental energisation of the metal trocar or access port.
tmj_page.rfmax_desc2: At CoworkDoc we developed a radiofrequency probe specifically for this procedure. With a diameter of 1.8mm and a 9cm working length, the rf-max navigates the confined space of the TMJ. Its blunt, atraumatic tip reduces the risk of perforation and tissue tearing. Only two poles are energised, preventing contact with the metal trocar. The active tip is 1.5mm long, facilitating precise cutting and coagulation.
tmj_page.rfmax_b1: 1.8mm diameter — designed specifically for the confined TMJ space
tmj_page.rfmax_b2: Blunt, atraumatic tip that reduces the risk of perforation and tissue tearing
tmj_page.rfmax_b3: Non-energised outer tube prevents accidental charge on the metal access trocar
tmj_page.rfmax_b4: 1.5mm active electrode length for precise operability within the cavity
tmj_page.rfmax_b5: Bipolar plasma radiofrequency — controlled energy delivery with minimal thermal dispersion
tmj_page.rfmax_b6: Designed from scratch with leading maxillofacial surgeons — not an adapted device
tmj_page.clinical_p1: The rf-max is designed specifically for temporomandibular joint surgery. Unlike devices adapted from other procedures, it was engineered from scratch based on direct input from leading maxillofacial surgeons. The result is a probe unique on the market, enabling precise cuts, reliable coagulation, and appropriate tissue handling within the joint cavity.
tmj_page.clinical_p2: The probe consists of three metal components separated by PTFE plastic. The outer metal tube carries no electrical charge, preventing accidental energy transmission to the access sheath. The central and tip electrodes are the active energy-emitting elements. The tip electrode is 1.5mm long, giving the surgeon greater operability within the joint space. Its blunt tip — a feature specific to TMJ surgery — reduces the risk of perforation and tissue tearing.
tmj_page.why_desc: Every CoworkDoc instrument begins with a surgeon describing an unmet need. We listen, interpret, and engineer solutions that don't exist anywhere else on the market.
tmj_page.why_card1_desc: The rf-max was developed with maxillofacial surgeons who identified specific limitations of existing TMJ instruments. Their clinical requirements drove every design decision.
tmj_page.why_card2_desc: Manufactured at an ISO 13485 certified, FDA-registered facility in Costa Rica — the same industrial zone as leading multinational medical device companies.
tmj_page.why_card3_desc: Distributed worldwide by Matching Deals — a multilingual commercial team managing the supply chain and regional availability of all CoworkDoc products.
tmj_page.cta_heading: Interested in the rf-max for your practice?
tmj_page.cta_body: Contact us for product information, pricing, and availability in your region. We respond within 1 business day.
tmj_page.more_orl_desc: Turbilon, rf-hook, and Bisuctor — three instruments for nasal surgery, skull base, sleep surgery, and larynx.
tmj_page.toast_msg: Interested in using this product in your practice?

about.title: Working side by side with surgeons
about.body_1: Coworkdoc means co-working with doctors. We are a Chilean research and development company focused on surgical medical devices. We listen to surgeons, interpret their requirements, and engineer solutions that don't exist anywhere else on the market.
about.pillar_1: Better quality of life for patients through purpose-built surgical instruments
about.pillar_2: Working together with surgeons to improve medical techniques
about.pillar_3: Aligned with the needs of diverse health systems across Latin America
about.iso_badge: ISO 13485 certified manufacturing · FDA registered facility
about.mission_quote: We listen. We interpret. We engineer.
about.mission_body_2: Every instrument we develop begins with a conversation — a surgeon describing an unmet clinical need, a procedure limited by inadequate tools, an outcome that could be improved. We translate those conversations into instruments designed specifically for that purpose.
about.coworkdoc_desc: Designs specifications, plans and surgical indications for all products
about.itek_role: Manufacturing · Cartago, Costa Rica (free trade zone)
about.itek_desc: ISO 13485 certified · FDA registered · Same zone as leading multinationals
about.matching_desc: Manages supply chain and distribution across Latin America
about.kol_desc: CoworkDoc instruments are designed in direct collaboration with practising ENT and maxillofacial surgeons.
about.cta_desc: Are you a surgeon with an unmet clinical need? We want to hear from you.

blog.title: Surgical Education & Clinical Insights
blog.desc: Resources for ENT and maxillofacial surgeons
blog.article_1_excerpt: Radiofrequency ablation of the inferior turbinate has emerged as a minimally invasive option for managing turbinate hypertrophy. This article reviews the technique and outcomes.
blog.article_2_title: TMJ Arthroscopy: Technique Overview with the rf-max Probe
blog.article_2_excerpt: The rf-max probe's 1.8mm diameter and blunt tip make it uniquely suited for the confined space of the temporomandibular joint. A step-by-step technique overview.
blog.article_3_title: Bipolar Plasma RF in Nasal Surgery: Clinical Outcomes
blog.article_3_excerpt: A review of bipolar plasma radiofrequency applications in nasal surgery, with focus on tissue preservation and reduced post-operative recovery times.
blog.article_4_title: Tongue Base Surgery for Snoring: The Basec Protocol
blog.article_4_excerpt: The Basec flexible-tip probe enables precise RF delivery to the tongue base with improved suction. This article outlines the surgical protocol.
blog.article_5_title: ISO 13485 in Latin America: Why Certification Matters
blog.article_5_excerpt: Understanding the significance of ISO 13485 certification for surgical device procurement in Latin American health systems.
blog.article_6_title: Collaborating with Surgeons: The CoworkDoc Development Process
blog.article_6_excerpt: How CoworkDoc's co-development model — working directly alongside ENT and maxillofacial surgeons — produces surgical instruments that address real unmet clinical needs.
blog.disclaimer: Educational content for qualified healthcare professionals only.

contact.title: More information
contact.desc: Send us a message and our team will get back to you about our products and surgical video library.
contact.details_intro: Reach us by email, phone, or WhatsApp. Our office is in Santiago, Chile and we cover all of Latin America.
contact.channels_desc: Prefer to reach us directly? Get in touch whichever way works best for you.
contact.channel_email_desc: Send us a message — we respond within 1 business day.
contact.channel_wa_desc: For quick questions and faster responses, message us directly.
contact.channel_phone_desc: Call us during office hours, Monday to Friday 9am–6pm CLT.
contact.faq_q1: Are the surgical videos available to all healthcare professionals?
contact.faq_a1: Yes — the surgical video library is available to all qualified healthcare professionals. No login is required. Content is intended exclusively for educational purposes and is not suitable for the general public or patients.
contact.faq_q2: In which countries are CoworkDoc products available?
contact.faq_a2: CoworkDoc products are distributed throughout Latin America by Matching Deals. For country-specific availability and pricing, contact us directly via the form, WhatsApp, or email.
contact.faq_q3: What certifications do CoworkDoc products hold?
contact.faq_a3: All products are manufactured by Itek Soluciones Integrales at an ISO 13485 certified, FDA-registered facility in Cartago, Costa Rica — ensuring products meet international medical device manufacturing standards.
contact.faq_q4: I am a surgeon with an unmet clinical need. Can CoworkDoc help?
contact.faq_a4: Absolutely — that is exactly how CoworkDoc works. Every instrument began with a surgeon identifying an unmet need. Contact us and we will arrange an initial conversation.
contact.faq_q5: I am interested in distributing CoworkDoc products in my country.
contact.faq_a5: Distribution enquiries are welcome. Use the contact form, select "Distribution inquiry", or email us directly at ventas@coworkdoc.cl.
contact.dist_surgeons_title: Interested in a product for your practice?
contact.dist_surgeons_desc: View our surgical video demonstrations and contact us for product information, pricing, and regional availability.
contact.dist_distributors_desc: We are actively expanding distribution across Latin America. If you represent a medical device distributor or hospital procurement network, we'd like to hear from you.
contact.form_privacy: Your information is handled in accordance with our
contact.form_success_msg: Thank you for reaching out. A member of our team will be in touch within 1 business day.
contact.form_error_email: Please enter a valid email address
